titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/245

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=39 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.719839+00 | translation moa=40 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.698654+00

div.p:
Alex’ . dei gratia Rex scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue Clericis ⁊ Laicis  Salutem . Sciant
presentes ⁊ futuri nos concessisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse donationem illam quam Iohannes de
Normanuill’ . fecit deo ⁊ Monachis de Melros’ de illa parte terre in territorio de Makeston’ . que
continetur infra Rectas diuisas contentas in carta ipsius Iohannis de Normanuill’ . Tenendam . et habendam .
predictis Monachis de Melros’ . in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam cum libero ingressu ⁊
egressu et transitu ipsis Monachis et Aueriis et vecturis suis et familie sue ad predictam terram
per terram predicti Iohannis de Makeston’ . Ita libere et quiete . plenarie et honorifice  sicut Car
ta predicti Iohannis inde facta predictis Monachis iuste testatur  saluo seruicio nostro . Testibus . Thoma . de
striuelin’ . Cancellario . Waltero . Olifard’ . Iustic’ . Laodon’ . Henrico . de Balliol’ . Camerario . Roberto . filio .
Roberti . de Ros’ . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Iohanne de vallibus . Willelmo de veteri ponte . Bernardo de Hauden’ . Alexandro
. de Synton’ . Apud Seleschr’ . xxij . die Iulii Anno Regni nostri duodecimo .

Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that we have granted and by this our charter have made firm the donation that John de Normanville made to God and to the monks of Melrose of that part of the land in the territory of Maxton which is contained within the correct boundaries described in John de Normanville’s own charter. For the aforesaid monks of Melrose to hold and have in free and pure and perpetual alms with free entrance and exit and transit for the monks themselves and for their work animals and wagons and household to the aforesaid land through the land of the aforesaid John at Maxton, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforesaid John made for the monks thereupon justly declares, saving our service. As witnesses: Thomas of Stirling, chancellor, Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian, Henry de Balliol, chamberlain, Robert son of Robert de Roos, John of Maxwell, John de Vaux, William de Vieuxpont, Bernard of Howden, Alexander of Synton. At Selkirk, on the 22nd day of July in the twelfth year of our reign.
